cases.description: Vector form exact match against an ascending-sorted lookup_vector
cases.expected_note: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/lookup-function-446d94af-663b-451d-8251-369d5e3864cb -- lookup_vector must be in ascending order for LOOKUP to work correctly; this vector is sorted

cases.description: No exact match; LOOKUP uses the largest lookup_vector value that is <= lookup_value
cases.expected_note: 3.5 has no exact match; largest value <=3.5 is 3 -> paired result 30

cases.description: lookup_value smaller than every entry in lookup_vector -> #N/A
Array form (single 2-D argument, no separate result_vector): since A1:B3 has more rows than columns, LOOKUP searches the FIRST column and returns from the LAST column
cases.expected_note: Per Microsoft docs' array-form rule (searches first row/col depending on which is longer): match on A2=2 returns paired last-column value 200
